description: A discovery catalog of citizen-facing government processes in the United States (US), the United Kingdom of Great Britain (GB), Ireland (IE), Canada (CA), New Zealand (NZ), Australia (AU), Germany (DE), Singapore (SG), France (FR), the Netherlands (NL), India (IN), and South Africa (ZA) that an AI agent would commonly carry out on behalf of an individual. Each entry is a candidate for a future GovSchema document, proposed against the registry id convention in registry/README.md. This is a planning artifact, not a registry of published schemas.

scope.audience: Individuals (the 'human companions' an agent acts for). Excludes purely business-internal or government-internal processes except where an individual routinely files them (e.g. sole-trader tax, single-member company formation).
scope.boundary: GovSchema describes a process so an agent can collect and validate the right inputs. It never submits a form, stores anyone's answers, or implies government endorsement. Catalog entries inherit that boundary.

disclaimer: A 'candidate' is a discovery proposal only. It becomes a GovSchema document solely after it is authored against its live government source, validated against spec/v0.1/govschema.schema.json, and given a verification record. 'candidateSource' URLs are starting points to be confirmed at authoring time, not verified sources. Entries with status 'published' link to schemas already in registry/.

tiers.1: High-frequency, well-bounded individual processes with a clear single source form. Strongest next candidates to author.
tiers.2: Common but multi-path, longer, or multi-document processes. Author after tier 1; expect richer flow modelling.
tiers.3: High-value but complex, sensitive, eligibility-heavy, or sub-nationally fragmented (e.g. immigration, means-tested benefits, state-administered programs). Require extra verification rigor and may need several schemas per jurisdiction.

fields.proposedId: Proposed registry id (registry/<id>/<version>/schema.json). Subject to change until authored.
fields.title: Human-readable working title.
fields.jurisdiction: country (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2) and level (national | subnational | municipal | supranational).
fields.authority: Government body that owns the process.
fields.processType: One of the spec process.type values: application, registration, renewal, amendment, filing, request, other.
fields.candidateSource: Candidate live source URL to confirm at authoring time.
fields.documentRef: Source form/reference number, where one exists.
fields.agentTask: What an agent would do for the individual.
fields.version: SemVer of the authored schema; present on published entries.
fields.edition: For time-versioned forms only (spec v0.2 §5.7): the edition.label (e.g. a tax year like '2025'). When present, the published schema lives at registry/<proposedId>/<edition>/<version>/schema.json.

candidates.title: United States Individual Income Tax Return (Form 1040)
candidates.agentTask: Prepare an individual federal income tax return.
candidates.notes: Reference schema (1040 core, pages 1-2 plus common line-level detail). Authored in registry/us/irs/individual-income-tax-return-1040/2025/1.0.0 (GOV-54, merged in PR #8) but the catalog entry was left at candidate — the edition-aware discovery/check.mjs (from GOV-49) was not yet confirmed working when GOV-54 landed. Flipped to published during the GOV-105 catalog audit (2026-07-01); no schema content changed. Exercises the v0.2 edition axis (edition.scheme us-tax-year, label 2025). Schedules are separate documents, out of scope here. Status draft pending full field-by-field source review.

candidates.title: United States Automatic Extension of Time to File (Form 4868)
candidates.agentTask: Request an automatic extension to file a federal income tax return.
candidates.notes: Reference schema. Authored in registry/us/irs/extension-to-file-4868/2025/1.0.0 (GOV-49). First GovSchema document to exercise the v0.2 edition/tax-year axis (edition.scheme us-tax-year, label 2025); the year is the edition, not part of id or a version bump, and editions coexist (see schema and VERIFICATION.md). Small, well-bounded slice of the IRS surface ahead of the full 1040. Status draft pending full field-by-field source review.

candidates.title: United States Employer Identification Number Application (Form SS-4)

candidates.notes: Individual-relevant for sole traders and single-member entities; opens the Business Formation vertical's federal identity-identifier candidate (GOV-282), the US counterpart to the tax-identifier family already covered by gb/hmrc/national-insurance-number-application, ie/dsp/pps-number-application, ca/esdc/social-insurance-number-application, nz/dia/realme-verified-identity, au/ato/tax-file-number-application, and de/finanzamt/tax-identification-number. Not time-versioned (GSP-0005 §2 coexistence test fails: the form isn't filed per tax year), so authored at the plain v0.1 path rather than the edition axis. Schema status 'draft' pending full field-by-field source review; see VERIFICATION.md.

candidates.title: United States Application for Naturalization (Form N-400)

candidates.title: United States Postal Service Change of Address

candidates.title: United States Selective Service Registration

candidates.notes: Authored in registry/us/sss/selective-service-registration/1.0.0 (GOV-44). Legally required for most people assigned male at birth ages 18-25; small fixed field set. candidates.title: United States Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA)

candidates.title: United States VA Health Care Application (Form 10-10EZ)

candidates.notes: Authored in registry/us/va/health-benefits-enrollment-10-10ez (GOV-926), sourced from the current (FEB 2025) fillable VA Form 10-10EZ PDF, directly downloadable from va.gov with no bot-block. A genuine AcroForm/XFA PDF: fields were cross-referenced against the PDF's own form-field annotations (name+type via pdfjs-dist getAnnotations), giving unusually direct field-name traceability. Models one spouse and one dependent child inline, matching the base form's printed capacity.

candidates.title: United States Global Entry (Trusted Traveler) Application

candidates.notes: Includes a conditional interview/appointment step; analogous to GB Registered Traveller.

candidates.title: California REAL ID Application

candidates.title: New York Driver License Renewal

authority.name: New York State Department of Motor Vehicles

candidates.agentTask: Renew an individual's New York driver license.
candidates.notes: Second-state copy of a license renewal — proves the format generalizes across US subdivisions and surfaces cross-state field differences. Published as registry/us/ny/dmv/drivers-license-renewal/1.0.0 (GOV-585).

candidates.title: California Voter Registration
candidates.agentTask: Register an eligible individual to vote in California.
candidates.notes: Voter registration is state-administered in the US; the federal vote.gov entry only routes to state systems. Model per-state, not nationally. Published as registry/us/ca/sos/voter-registration/1.0.0 (GOV-722), authored by directly operating the live covr.sos.ca.gov wizard (a server-rendered ASP.NET application, not a JS SPA) through Step 4 of 5 (Review), stopping before final Submit. Companion to au/aec/voter-enrolment (GOV-707): closes the US/CA half of this vertical's voter-registration pair.

candidates.title: California LLC Formation (Articles of Organization, Form LLC-1)
candidates.agentTask: Form a single-member LLC in California for an individual.
candidates.notes: Completes the 'LLC' leg of the Business Formation vertical named in GOV-282, alongside the federal identifier leg (us/irs/employer-identification-number-ss4) and gb/companies-house/company-incorporation-in01's UK counterpart. Authored in registry/us/ca/sos/business-entity-llc-formation/1.0.0 from the Form LLC-1 (Rev. 11/2023) PDF (retrieved via a Wayback Machine capture after the live CDN URL returned 403 to direct fetch) plus the LLC-1 Instructions (Rev. 08/2019) for the name/agent/signature rules not restated on the form. Not time-versioned (GSP-0005 §2 coexistence test fails: Articles of Organization is a one-time filing), so authored at the plain v0.1-style (non-edition) path under the v0.2 meta-schema. Schema status 'draft' pending full field-by-field source review; flags one open discrepancy between the 2019 and 2023 source revisions over whether the principal office must be in California — see VERIFICATION.md.

candidates.title: California General Stock Corporation Incorporation (Articles of Incorporation, Form ARTS-GS)
candidates.agentTask: Incorporate a single-class-of-stock general stock corporation in California for an individual founder.
candidates.notes: Completes the 'Incorporation' leg of the Business Formation vertical named in GOV-282, alongside the LLC leg (us/ca/sos/business-entity-llc-formation) and the federal identifier leg (us/irs/employer-identification-number-ss4) — added directly at 'published' since it did not previously exist as a candidate (this cycle's phase-2 research found it missing rather than picking an existing backlog item). Authored in registry/us/ca/sos/corporation-formation-arts-gs/1.0.0 from the Form ARTS-GS (Rev. 06/2023) PDF (retrieved via a Wayback Machine capture after the live CDN URL returned 403 to direct fetch) plus the ARTS-GS Instructions (Rev. 06/2019) for the agent/shares/signature rules not restated on the form. Not time-versioned (GSP-0005 §2 coexistence test fails: Articles of Incorporation is a one-time filing), so authored at the plain v0.1-style (non-edition) path under the v0.2 meta-schema. Schema status 'draft' pending full field-by-field source review; see VERIFICATION.md.

candidates.title: California Vehicle Title Transfer and Replacement (Form REG 227)
candidates.agentTask: Transfer a vehicle's Certificate of Title from a seller to a new individual owner when the original paper title is unavailable (lost, stolen, illegible, or never received).
candidates.notes: Opens the 'Title' leg of the DMV vertical named in GOV-282 (Tag, Title, Registration, Drivers License, IDL, CDL) — Registration renewal (us/ca/dmv/vehicle-registration-renewal) and Drivers License renewal (us/ca/dmv/drivers-license-renewal) were already published, but Title, CDL, and IDL had no candidates at all. Added directly at 'published' since it did not previously exist as a candidate. Authored in registry/us/ca/dmv/vehicle-title-transfer/1.0.0 from the live Form REG 227 (Rev. 9/2021) PDF at dmv.ca.gov (no CDN block encountered here, unlike the SOS bpd.cdn.sos.ca.gov forms). Scoped to an individual-to-individual vehicle transfer (Sections 0-1-2-3-4-6-7); the notarized legal-owner lien-release path (Section 5) and the dealer's-release path (Section 8) are out of scope, as are vessels/vessel-specific fields. Not time-versioned (GSP-0005 §2 coexistence test fails: a title transfer isn't filed 'for' a recurring period), so authored at the plain v0.1-style (non-edition) path under the v0.2 meta-schema. Schema status 'draft' pending full field-by-field source review; see VERIFICATION.md.

candidates.title: California Commercial Driver License (CDL) Application
candidates.agentTask: Prepare a California Commercial Learner's Permit (CLP) / Commercial Driver's License (CDL) application: class, endorsements, self-certified commerce type, prior-licence history, and medical certification.
candidates.notes: Closes the 'CDL' gap of the DMV vertical named in GOV-282 (Tag, Title, Registration, Drivers License, IDL, CDL) — Registration renewal, Drivers License renewal, and Title transfer/replacement were already published, and CDL had no candidate at all (GOV-381 discovery pass). IDL (International Driving Permit) remains an open gap: in the US it is issued by AAA/AATA under State Department authorization rather than directly by a government motor-vehicle authority, so it needs a standards decision on whether a treaty-authorized third-party issuer fits the `authority` model before it can be authored (flag for the Founding Engineer). Added directly at 'published' since CDL did not previously exist as a candidate. Authored in registry/us/ca/dmv/commercial-drivers-license-application/1.0.0 from the CA DMV CDL overview and commercial driver handbook §1, plus the federal endorsement/restriction codes (49 CFR 383.153) and self-certification categories (49 CFR 383.71/391.11) that California's program incorporates by reference. Scoped to the CLP/CDL application intake only (DL 44C); the subsequent knowledge/skills testing, 14-day CLP waiting period, ELDT provider verification, and CDL issuance are out of scope. Not time-versioned (GSP-0005 §2 coexistence test fails: a CDL application isn't filed per recurring period), so authored at the plain v0.1 path. Schema status 'draft' pending full field-by-field source review; see VERIFICATION.md.

candidates.title: United States Statement Regarding a Valid Lost or Stolen Passport Book and/or Card (Form DS-64)

authority.name: U.S. Department of State, Bureau of Consular Affairs

candidates.agentTask: Report an individual's valid U.S. passport book and/or card as lost or stolen, typically alongside a new passport application.
candidates.notes: Opens the 'Lost' leg of the Passport vertical named in GOV-282 (First-Time, Renewal, Lost, etc.) — eight passport documents across seven jurisdictions were already published (all first-time/renewal), but no 'Lost' candidate existed at all, even in the backlog. Added directly at 'published' since it did not previously exist as a candidate. Authored in registry/us/dos/lost-or-stolen-passport-ds64/1.0.0 from the live Form DS-64 PDF at eforms.state.gov (fetched directly, no access block, consistent with the sibling us/dos/passport-application-ds11 and us/dos/passport-renewal-ds82 documents). Not time-versioned (GSP-0005 §2 coexistence test fails: a lost/stolen report is a one-time filing per incident), so authored at the plain v0.1-style (non-edition) path under the v0.2 meta-schema. Schema status 'draft' pending full field-by-field source review; see VERIFICATION.md.

candidates.title: United States Application for a U.S. Passport for Eligible Individuals — Correction, Name Change, and Limited Passport Replacement (Form DS-5504)

authority.name: U.S. Department of State, Bureau of Consular Affairs

candidates.agentTask: Reapply for a U.S. passport book and/or card to correct a data or printing error, change a name within one year of both a legal name change and the current passport's issuance, or replace a limited-validity passport, at no cost unless expedited.
candidates.notes: Completes the U.S. Department of State passport form family named across GOV-282/GOV-664: us/dos/passport-application-ds11 (first-time/replacement), us/dos/passport-renewal-ds82 (routine mail-in renewal), and us/dos/lost-or-stolen-passport-ds64 (lost/stolen report) were already published; DS-5504 (GOV-678, GOV-664 plan §2 F1.8) was the one remaining sibling. Added directly at 'published' since it did not previously exist as a candidate. Authored in registry/us/dos/passport-name-change-ds5504/1.0.0 from the live 'Change or Correct a Passport' page and the live fillable Form DS-5504 PDF at eforms.state.gov (fetched directly, no access block), extracted both via pdfjs-dist page text and pdf-lib AcroForm field enumeration, the same two-source method used for us/dos/passport-renewal-ds82 v2.0.0. Unlike DS-82's 'answer Yes to ALL' eligibility gate, DS-5504's Section A is an 'answer Yes to ANY of three' gate, modelled as three independent fieldRole:eligibility booleans. Not time-versioned (GSP-0005 §2 coexistence test fails: a one-time name-change/correction filing isn't tied to a recurring period), so authored at the plain (non-edition) path. Schema status 'draft' pending full independent field-by-field re-verification; see VERIFICATION.md.

authority.name: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Food and Nutrition Service

candidates.agentTask: Apply for SNAP (food) benefits.
candidates.notes: Federally funded but state-administered: each state has its own application. The national entry is informational; real schemas live at subnational level (e.g. us/ca/...). Flagged here so the fragmentation is recorded, not assumed away.

candidates.agentTask: Renew a 10-year photocard driving licence.
candidates.notes: Reference schema. Published in registry/gb/dvla/driving-licence-renewal-photocard/1.0.0 (GOV-185).

candidates.title: United Kingdom First Provisional Driving Licence
candidates.agentTask: Apply for a first provisional driving licence.
candidates.notes: Fills the 'Drivers License' first-time gap in the DMV vertical named in GOV-282/GOV-419: every previously published driver-licensing schema (this jurisdiction's own gb/dvla/driving-licence-renewal-photocard, plus us/ca/dmv, ie/dttas) was a renewal, none a first application. Authored in registry/gb/dvla/driving-licence-first-provisional/1.0.0 (GOV-419) from the live gov.uk 'Apply for your first provisional driving licence' and 'Proving your identity for a driving licence' pages. The equivalent online service's pre-login screens (readable for the sibling renewal schema) 404'd during this pass, and no official DVLA field-by-field D1 form specification was found, so scope is deliberately narrow: eligibility (age/residency/eyesight), channel/payment, identity-verification method, a medical-declaration gate, and confirmation email. Biographic detail (sex, address, previous surname), photo capture, and licence categories applied for are out of scope pending a future review pass; see VERIFICATION.md. Not time-versioned (GSP-0005 §2 coexistence test fails), so authored at the plain v1.0.0 path under spec v0.3. Schema status 'draft' pending full field-by-field source review; a conformance/gb/dvla/driving-licence-first-provisional/1.0.0/application-packet.json mock test run passed the schema's own required/requiredWhen/validation constraints.

candidates.title: United Kingdom Self Assessment Tax Return (SA100)
candidates.agentTask: File an individual's Self Assessment tax return.

candidates.title: United Kingdom National Insurance Number Application

authority.name: His Majesty's Revenue and Customs / Department for Work and Pensions

candidates.agentTask: Apply for a National Insurance number.
candidates.notes: GOV-926 research: the online application service (apply-national-insurance-number.service.gov.uk) hangs/times out on both direct fetch and headless Playwright (IP-level block, not a JS challenge — same class as gov.au domains). Wayback Machine only has the 'service unavailable' placeholder page cached for the actual per-question pages (page path slugs survive in the CDX index and reveal the wizard step sequence, but not real field labels/options) — insufficient to source field content faithfully without fabricating detail. A future cycle could retry from different network egress, or treat the page-slug sequence as a starting skeleton to verify against a live walkthrough once reachable.

candidates.title: United Kingdom Private Limited Company Incorporation (IN01)

candidates.notes: First GovSchema company-formation reference schema in any jurisdiction (GOV-179); GB counterpart to US state LLC formation, nationwide single authority unlike the US. Authored in registry/gb/companies-house/company-incorporation-in01/1.0.0 from the current IN01 PDF (v12.0, Feb 2026). Scoped to the single-director/single-subscriber/single-share-class/single-PSC private-limited-by-shares case, since v0.2's flat field model has no repeating-group construct for the form's continuation-page sections (additional directors/subscribers/PSCs, multiple share classes/currencies); corporate directors/secretaries, guarantee/unlimited/public companies, and RLE/ORP PSCs are likewise out of scope. Schema status 'draft' pending full field-by-field source review of the live register-a-company-online screens; see VERIFICATION.md.

candidates.title: Ireland Adult Passport Renewal (Passport Online)

authority.name: Department of Foreign Affairs, Passport Service

candidates.agentTask: Renew an eligible adult's Irish passport (book or card) through Passport Online.
candidates.notes: First IE reference schema (GOV-105); opens a third jurisdiction alongside US and GB. Authored in registry/ie/dfa/passport-renewal-adult/1.0.0. Since 1 December 2025 the paper APS1/APS2 forms are no longer accepted, so (like gb/dvla/vehicle-tax) there is no downloadable form; transcribed from DFA/citizensinformation.ie guidance prose, not an independently captured screen flow. GB/US counterpart to gb/hmpo/passport-renewal-adult and us/dos/passport-renewal-ds82. Status draft pending full field-by-field source review; see VERIFICATION.md for open items (notably whether a delivery address is separately collected).

candidates.title: Ireland First Adult Passport Application

authority.name: Department of Foreign Affairs, Passport Service

candidates.agentTask: Apply for a first Irish adult passport.
candidates.notes: Authored in registry/ie/dfa/passport-application-first-adult/1.0.0 (GOV-120). Sibling to the published renewal; adds identity witnessing by An Garda Siochana (in-State) or an appropriate witness abroad, citizenship-evidence branching (birth cert / naturalisation cert / Foreign Birth Registration cert), and documentary proof of address/name. Also the fallback path for a passport issued more than 15 years ago, out of scope in the renewal schema. Schema status 'draft' pending full field-by-field source review; see VERIFICATION.md.

candidates.title: Ireland Driving Licence Renewal (NDLS)

authority.name: Road Safety Authority, National Driver Licence Service

candidates.agentTask: Renew an individual's Irish driving licence through the NDLS.
candidates.notes: Single nationwide authority (unlike US state-by-state DMVs); pairs with gb/dvla/driving-licence-renewal-photocard and us/ca/dmv/drivers-license-renewal to show one process across three jurisdictions. In-person photo/signature capture at an NDLS centre is part of the flow.

candidates.title: Ireland Personal Public Service (PPS) Number Application
candidates.agentTask: Apply for a PPS number, Ireland's tax/benefits/public-service identifier.
candidates.notes: GB counterpart to gb/hmrc/national-insurance-number-application; identity-verification driven, common for new arrivals. Referenced as an optional field (ppsNumber) in the published passport renewal schema. Authored in registry/ie/dsp/pps-number-application/1.0.0 (GOV-200). The original candidateSource path ('how-to-apply-for-a-personal-public-service-pps-number') now 404s; corrected here to the live 'get-a-personal-public-service-pps-number' page, which was the source actually used. Schema status 'draft' pending full field-by-field source review against the authenticated MyWelfare screens; see VERIFICATION.md.

candidates.title: Ireland Private Company Limited by Shares Incorporation (Form A1)
candidates.agentTask: Incorporate a single-member private limited company (LTD) in Ireland.
candidates.notes: Third company-formation jurisdiction alongside gb/companies-house/company-incorporation-in01 and us/ca/sos/business-entity-llc-formation; nationwide single authority like GB, unlike the US. Authored in registry/ie/cro/company-incorporation/1.0.0 from cro.ie's Required Steps, Company Incorporation (Leaflet 1), Company Officers, Registered Office, Company Name, and Registration Methods pages (via Wayback Machine snapshots; cro.ie 403s to direct fetch), corroborated against cpdstore.ie's Form A1 field-list explainer. Scoped to the single-director/single-secretary/single-subscriber LTD case, matching the precedent set by the GB and US-CA company-formation schemas. Open item: whether PPSN is now mandatory on Form A1 itself (vs. only the separate RBO filing) is unconfirmed against the live CORE form; see VERIFICATION.md.

candidates.title: Canada Adult Simplified Renewal Passport Application (Form PPTC 054)

authority.name: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada, Passport Program

candidates.agentTask: Renew an eligible adult's Canadian passport using the no-guarantor Simplified Renewal process.
candidates.notes: First CA reference schema (GOV-132); opens a fourth jurisdiction alongside US, GB, and IE. Authored in registry/ca/ircc/passport-renewal-simplified/1.0.0. canada.ca returned HTTP 403 to automated retrieval of every page and the form PDF, so (unlike prior passport schemas) this one is built from independently cross-checked secondary transcriptions rather than a direct primary-source read; see VERIFICATION.md for the access-constraint note and a resolved source conflict over whether name-change renewals are accepted (they are not). Status draft pending direct-access field-by-field source review.

candidates.title: Canada First Adult Passport Application (Form PPTC 153)

authority.name: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada, Passport Program

candidates.agentTask: Apply for a first Canadian adult passport, or renew when ineligible for Simplified Renewal (e.g. a name change).
candidates.notes: Sibling to the published Simplified Renewal; adds guarantor/reference requirements and citizenship-evidence branching. Also the fallback path noted in ca/ircc/passport-renewal-simplified's VERIFICATION.md for applicants whose personal details have changed. GOV-282: authored in registry/ca/ircc/passport-application-first-adult/1.0.0 from the live Form PPTC 153 PDF at canada.ca, fetched directly with no access block (unlike the sibling Simplified Renewal, which hit a 403 when it was authored). Models the 9-section application form; open-ended repeating structures (a second identity document, the address/occupation history in section 7) are out of scope (GSP-0009). Not time-versioned (GSP-0005 §2 coexistence test fails: a first passport is a one-time filing per document), so authored at the plain v0.1-style (non-edition) path under the v0.2 meta-schema. Schema status 'draft' pending full field-by-field source review; see VERIFICATION.md.

candidates.title: Canada Social Insurance Number (SIN) Application

authority.name: Employment and Social Development Canada, Service Canada

candidates.agentTask: Apply for a Social Insurance Number, Canada's tax/benefits/employment identifier.
candidates.notes: GB/IE counterpart to gb/hmrc/national-insurance-number-application and ie/dsp/pps-number-application; identity-verification driven, common for new arrivals and school-leavers. GOV-282: authored in registry/ca/esdc/social-insurance-number-application/1.0.0 from Form GC-NAS2120, fetched directly from catalogue.servicecanada.gc.ca with no access block. Models the application form's own biographic/mailing fields (items 1-13); the status-dependent identity-document requirements (Step 1 of the Information Guide) are process guidance, not form fields, and are out of scope. Not time-versioned (GSP-0005 §2 coexistence test fails: a SIN is issued once per lifetime), so authored at the plain v0.1-style (non-edition) path under the v0.2 meta-schema. Schema status 'draft' pending full field-by-field source review; see VERIFICATION.md.

candidates.title: Ontario Driver's Licence Renewal
candidates.agentTask: Renew an individual's Ontario driver's licence.
candidates.notes: Driver licensing is provincial in Canada (like US state DMVs), unlike the single-authority GB/IE model; pairs with us/ca/dmv/drivers-license-renewal, gb/dvla/driving-licence-renewal-photocard, and ie/dttas/driving-licence-renewal to show one process across four jurisdictions. Published in GOV-483: registry/ca/on/mto/drivers-licence-renewal/1.0.0.

candidates.title: Ontario Business Corporation Incorporation (Articles of Incorporation)

authority.name: Ontario Business Registry (Ministry of Public and Business Service Delivery)

candidates.agentTask: Incorporate a single-shareholder business corporation in Ontario for an individual.
candidates.notes: Company formation is provincial (and federally, via Corporations Canada) in Canada; fourth company-formation jurisdiction alongside gb/companies-house/company-incorporation-in01, us/ca/sos/business-entity-llc-formation, and ie/cro/company-incorporation. Authored in registry/ca/on/registration/business-incorporation/1.0.0 (GOV-474): single-director, single-individual-incorporator scope; French/bilingual names, corporate incorporators, multi-director, and lot/concession addressing out of scope (see VERIFICATION.md).

candidates.title: Canada Federal Voter Registration (Online Voter Registration Service)
candidates.agentTask: Register an eligible Canadian citizen to vote in a federal election, or update an existing registration, in the National Register of Electors.
candidates.notes: GOV-960 research cycle: per-country x per-vertical audit found Canada was the only one of the four Commonwealth-pattern jurisdictions already covered elsewhere in this vertical (AU/AEC, GB/Cabinet Office, IN/ECI) without a federal voter-registration schema, matching the issue's own 'remaining voter registration' note. Published in registry/ca/elections-canada/voter-registration/1.0.0 (GOV-960): sourced from a live headless-browser walkthrough of ereg.elections.ca's public, unauthenticated wizard (mock data, stopped before the CAPTCHA/Submit screen). Civic (street-address) home-address path only; rural Section/Township/Range/Meridian and Lot & Concession address types, the Register of Future Electors (14-17) path, the International Register of Electors path for Canadians abroad, and the post-CAPTCHA submission outcome are out of scope (see VERIFICATION.md).

candidates.title: United Kingdom Register with a GP (GMS1)
candidates.agentTask: Register an individual with an NHS GP surgery.
candidates.notes: Health is devolved (England/Scotland/Wales/NI differ). Start with England GMS1; record the devolution split in jurisdiction notes.

candidates.title: New Zealand Adult Passport Renewal

authority.name: Department of Internal Affairs, New Zealand Passports

candidates.agentTask: Renew or replace an eligible adult's New Zealand passport through the DIA online passport service.
candidates.notes: First NZ reference schema (GOV-148); opens a fifth jurisdiction alongside US, GB, IE, and CA. Authored in registry/nz/dia/passport-renewal-adult/1.0.0. passports.govt.nz (the DIA's own domain) returned HTTP 403 to automated retrieval of its guidance pages and the PAS300/PAS310 PDFs, but govt.nz (the cross-agency government portal) served its pages directly, and one DIA PDF hosted on govt.nz (the BDM76 identity referee declaration) was read in full — better primary-source grounding than the CA IRCC schema, which had zero direct access. See VERIFICATION.md. Status draft pending direct PAS300 field-by-field review; single national authority, unlike the equivalent US/CA passport processes.

candidates.title: New Zealand First-Time Adult Passport Application

authority.name: Department of Internal Affairs, New Zealand Passports

candidates.agentTask: Prepare a first-time adult New Zealand passport application.
candidates.notes: GOV-1051 research cycle (2026-07-04): a per-jurisdiction x per-vertical catalog review found registry/nz/dia published only passport-renewal-adult and realme-verified-identity — no first-time application, unlike every other jurisdiction with a published passport document (US, GB, IE, AU, CA all have both). Added directly at 'published' since it did not previously exist as a candidate. Authored in registry/nz/dia/passport-application-first-adult/1.0.0 from the live govt.nz/dia.services.govt.nz guidance pages plus the full text of Form PAS310 (31 Jan 2025 edition), retrieved via an exact Wayback Machine capture of the live, otherwise-403-blocked passports.govt.nz PDF (CDX-API-confirmed URL match) and read verbatim with pdfjs-dist — stronger direct-source grounding than the sibling renewal schema, whose applicant-field section relied on secondary corroboration. Scoped to an applicant whose name has never changed since birth (PAS310 sections 6/7 name-change pathways gated out) and who completes their own application (section 8's statutory-declaration edge case out of scope). Not time-versioned (GSP-0005 §2 coexistence test fails: a first-time application isn't filed per period), so authored at the plain v1.0.0 path under spec v0.3. A mock application-packet.json conformance fixture (born-in-NZ path) and a second hand-checked branch (citizenship-by-descent + different delivery address) both passed the schema's own required/requiredWhen/validation rules via a scripted checker. Schema status 'draft' pending independent field-by-field re-verification; see VERIFICATION.md.

candidates.title: New Zealand Driver Licence Renewal
candidates.agentTask: Renew an individual's New Zealand driver licence.
candidates.notes: Single nationwide authority (unlike US/CA state-or-province DMVs), pairing with the GB/IE/CA driver-licensing entries. Renewal is completed in person at a driver licensing agent (a third-party network, not NZTA directly) using the same DL1 form NZTA uses for new and reissue applications; this schema models the renewal use of that form, including the DL26 identity-referee trigger for applicants without satisfactory photo ID. Published in GOV-497: registry/nz/nzta/drivers-licence-renewal/1.0.0. nzta.govt.nz blocked direct fetch in this sandbox (Incapsula WAF); sourced from a current (2026-05-25) Wayback Machine capture of the live 12/25-edition DL1 PDF. See VERIFICATION.md.

candidates.title: New Zealand RealMe Verified Identity Enrolment
candidates.agentTask: Enrol an individual for a RealMe verified identity, the credential that gates online passport, driver licence, and other NZ government service applications.
candidates.notes: Cross-cutting identity-verification service in the same family as gb/hmrc/national-insurance-number-application, ie/dsp/pps-number-application, and ca/esdc/social-insurance-number-application, but unlike those it is a credential/login rather than a tax/benefit identifier — referenced as a precondition in nz/dia/passport-renewal-adult rather than modelled inline there. Authored in registry/nz/dia/realme-verified-identity/1.0.0 (GOV-660) from DIA's own Identity Verification Service Privacy Statement, which names the exact personal-information fields collected per identity-record choice (NZ passport / birth certificate / citizenship certificate / immigration record), plus the main application-overview and photo/terms-of-use guidance pages. The live application form itself sits behind an authenticated RealMe login not reachable in this environment without genuine identity documents, so no live form walkthrough was performed — schema status remains 'draft'. See VERIFICATION.md.

candidates.title: Australia Adult Passport Renewal

authority.name: Australian Passport Office, Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade

candidates.agentTask: Renew an eligible adult's Australian passport for lodgement at a participating Australia Post outlet.
candidates.notes: Reference schema authored this cycle (GOV-162), the first AU entry in the catalog. Opens Australia as a sixth covered jurisdiction, chosen as the discovery gap this pass: no AU coverage existed despite AU being a natural next jurisdiction (national passport authority, English-language source, precedent in the sibling GB/IE/CA/NZ passport-renewal schemas).

candidates.title: Australia First Adult Passport Application

authority.name: Australian Passport Office, Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade

candidates.agentTask: Apply for a first Australian passport as an adult, including the citizenship-evidence and referee/guarantor identity checks that renewal (au/apo/passport-renewal-adult) does not require.
candidates.notes: Natural next AU schema, mirroring the renewal/first-application pairing already published for gb/hmpo and ie/dfa. Needs the referee/guarantor field cluster this cycle's renewal schema deliberately omitted, and the identity-document-category logic from identity-documents-you-need. Authored in registry/au/apo/passport-application-first-adult/1.0.0 (GOV-381 discovery pass, Passport vertical): citizenship-evidence, a simplified Category A/B vs. Category-C-fallback PID pathway, and online-application referee eligibility, layered onto the renewal schema's shared applicant/contact/photo/product fields. passports.gov.au blocked direct retrieval and a Wayback Machine fetch was also unavailable to this review's tooling (stricter than the renewal schema's access path), so sourcing leans on corroborated secondary consular-post/agent sources; see VERIFICATION.md. Schema status 'draft' pending full field-by-field source review.

candidates.title: Australia Tax File Number (TFN) Application
candidates.agentTask: Apply for a Tax File Number, the identity identifier gating Australian employment, tax, and benefit services.
candidates.notes: Authored in registry/au/ato/tax-file-number-application (GOV-926), modelling the standard paper application (NAT 1432) for an Australian resident who has never had a TFN. ato.gov.au 403-blocks all direct/automated fetches (confirmed still true); sourced instead from a Wayback Machine snapshot of the ATO site's own Next.js JSON data endpoint (confirms current channels + form number) plus a third-party mirror of the NAT 1432 paper form (edition 12.2005) for field-level content, following this registry's retired-form-mirror precedent. Completes the cross-jurisdiction identity-identifier family alongside gb/hmrc, ie/dsp, ca/esdc, and nz/dia.

candidates.title: Australia Medicare Enrolment
candidates.agentTask: Enrol an eligible individual in Medicare, Australia's national health-cover scheme.
candidates.notes: AU health registration is national (Medicare/Services Australia), unlike GB's devolved NHS GP registration (gb/register-with-a-gp candidate) — a structural contrast worth noting when this is authored.

candidates.title: New South Wales Driver Licence Renewal
candidates.agentTask: Renew a New South Wales driver licence online.
candidates.notes: Driver licensing in Australia is state/territory-administered, like the US (us/ca/dmv/drivers-license-renewal) and unlike GB's single national DVLA. NSW is the largest state by population; other states/territories would need their own schemas following the us/<state>/... precedent as au/<state>/.... Published in GOV-518: registry/au/nsw/service-nsw/driver-licence-renewal/1.0.0, scoped to full-licence (C/R/LR/MR/HR/HC/MC, single or dual-class-with-R) renewal only — provisional P1/P2 renewal/upgrade and the in-person-only 10-year term are out of scope. The original candidateSource URL (.../renew-your-driver-licence) now 404s; the current transaction page is .../renew-or-upgrade-a-nsw-driver-licence (updated above). See VERIFICATION.md.

candidates.title: New South Wales Vehicle Registration Renewal (Rego)
candidates.agentTask: Renew a New South Wales vehicle registration ('rego') online.
candidates.notes: Closes another leg of the gap GOV-960 first identified (7 of 11 jurisdictions have a Drivers-Licence schema but no Vehicle Registration/Tag/Title schema) and GOV-968 partly closed (ie/revenue/vehicle-registration-tax-vrt). AU, SG, CA, NZ, IN, NL remain open after this one. Added directly at 'published' since it did not previously exist as a candidate (this cycle's own catalog re-audit, GOV-979, found it missing rather than picking an existing backlog item) — pairs with the already-published au/nsw/service-nsw/driver-licence-renewal (same authority). Authored in registry/au/nsw/service-nsw/vehicle-registration-renewal/1.0.0 from the live service.nsw.gov.au guidance page (no downloadable form exists for this process); a headless-browser walkthrough reached the live registration-renewal.service.nsw.gov.au/find-vehicle vehicle-lookup screen and confirmed its single field, but a mock plate number returned a genuine 'Registration not found' server-side lookup result, the same class of gate that left ca/on/mto/vehicle-permit-renewal at 'candidate' rather than 'published' — see VERIFICATION.md.

candidates.title: Australia Voter Enrolment
candidates.agentTask: Enrol (or update enrolment details) on the Commonwealth electoral roll — enrolment is compulsory for eligible Australian citizens.
candidates.notes: GOV-707 (recurring standard-research cycle). Unlike the US, where voter registration is state-administered (a discovery README observation already recorded for us/*), AU electoral enrolment is a single national AEC roll, closer in shape to gb/co/register-to-vote — and unlike GB, enrolment is compulsory. Authored in registry/au/aec/voter-enrolment/1.0.0 from the AEC's downloadable fillable PDF enrolment form (ER016w, NSW edition), decoded field-by-field from the PDF's own AcroForm field names and static label text. Cross-checked against the Victoria edition of the same form: 78 of 79 AcroForm field names identical; the one difference (an occupation field) is NSW-specific and excluded. The live online wizard (enrol.aec.gov.au) could not be reached from this environment (DNS resolution failure, same class of block as nzta.govt.nz). Models new enrolment plus the form's own name-change and address-change branches; special-category enrolment (silent/overseas/itinerant/prisoner electors) is out of scope. Schema status 'draft' pending live-wizard confirmation; see VERIFICATION.md. Leaves us/ca/sos/voter-registration as the last open voter-registration candidate in this vertical.

candidates.title: Germany Residence Registration (Anmeldung)

authority.name: Meldebehörde (local registration office), under the Bundesmeldegesetz

candidates.agentTask: Register an individual's sole or main residence with the local Meldebehörde after moving into a German dwelling, within the legally required 14 days.
candidates.notes: First DE reference schema (GOV-170); opens a seventh jurisdiction and the first non-Anglophone, civil-law one — every prior jurisdiction (US, GB, IE, CA, NZ, AU) is Anglophone/common-law, a gap the discovery README had not yet addressed. Authored in registry/de/bmi/residence-registration/1.0.0. The Bundesmeldegesetz sets the registration data model nationally; Berlin's Bürgeramt service (dienstleistung 120686) is used as the concrete exemplar for the in-person channel, per the same convention as us/ca/dmv for US state DMVs, alongside the federal Elektronische Wohnsitzanmeldung (EWA) online channel. Scoped to a single adult registrant's sole/main-residence registration only; excludes Nebenwohnung-only registration, multi-person family co-registration, deregistration (Abmeldung), and the Hauptwohnungserklärung supplement. Schema status 'draft' pending full field-by-field source review, notably of the gender/marital-status enum option labels; see VERIFICATION.md.

candidates.title: Germany Residence Deregistration (Abmeldung)

authority.name: Meldebehörde (local registration office), under the Bundesmeldegesetz

candidates.agentTask: Deregister an individual's German residence, typically required only when moving abroad with no further German dwelling (BMG §17 Abs. 1).
candidates.notes: Sibling to the published de/bmi/residence-registration; deregistration is not required for a purely domestic move (a new Anmeldung supersedes the old address automatically), only for leaving Germany altogether or keeping no further German dwelling — confirmed directly from service.berlin.de at authoring time. GOV-282: authored in registry/de/bmi/residence-deregistration/1.0.0 from Berlin's official Abmeldung form PDF (fetched directly, no access block) plus the service page's own prose for the timing window, fee, legal basis, and submission-channel rules (in-writing only — no online EWA-equivalent channel, unlike registration). Scoped to a single adult registrant only (form supports up to 3 people per sheet). Not time-versioned (GSP-0005 §2 coexistence test fails: a deregistration is a one-time filing per move), so authored at the plain v0.1-style (non-edition) path under the v0.2 meta-schema. Schema status 'draft' pending full field-by-field source review, notably the gender enum (reused by analogy from the sibling schema, not independently re-confirmed against this PDF); see VERIFICATION.md.

candidates.title: Germany Passport Application/Renewal (Reisepass)

authority.name: Meldebehörde/Passbehörde (local passport office), under the Passgesetz (PassG)

candidates.agentTask: Apply for or renew a German passport at the local passport office (in person only; unlike the online-eligible residence registration).
candidates.notes: Federal law (Passgesetz), locally administered like residence registration — same jurisdiction-modelling pattern as de/bmi/residence-registration. GB/IE/CA/NZ/AU counterpart; would be DE's first passport schema and, unlike those, is in-person-only (no online submission channel). GOV-504: authored in registry/de/bmi/passport-application/1.0.0 from Berlin's official Reisepass service page (dienstleistung 121151), cross-checked against amtlich-einfach.de for national representativeness. This closes the last open Passport-vertical candidate (US/GB/IE/CA/NZ/AU/SG/DE all now published).

candidates.title: Germany Vehicle Registration (Kfz-Zulassung)
candidates.agentTask: Register a vehicle (or i-Kfz online re-registration) with the local Zulassungsstelle.
candidates.notes: Reference schema. Already in registry/de/kba/vehicle-registration/1.0.0. The original candidateSource (kba.de/DE/Themen/ZFZR/zfzr_node.html) now 404s; corrected to the BMV (Bundesministerium für Verkehr) i-Kfz article, corroborated against the KBA's own i-Kfz page. Scoped to two of the five i-Kfz process types (new registration and same-owner re-registration); see VERIFICATION.md for the Umschreibung/Abmeldung/Tageszulassung scope line.

candidates.title: Germany Tax Identification Number (Steuer-Identifikationsnummer)

authority.name: Bundeszentralamt für Steuern (Federal Central Tax Office)

candidates.agentTask: Retrieve or request re-issuance of an individual's Steuer-ID, Germany's lifetime tax identifier issued automatically after residence registration.
candidates.notes: Cross-cutting identity-identifier service in the same family as gb/hmrc/national-insurance-number-application, ie/dsp/pps-number-application, ca/esdc/social-insurance-number-application, nz/dia/realme-verified-identity, and au/ato/tax-file-number-application — every covered jurisdiction now has one such candidate. Distinct from those: the Steuer-ID is issued automatically (via post) after an Anmeldung rather than applied for, so this schema models the retrieval/re-issuance request, not an application. Authored in registry/de/finanzamt/tax-identification-number/1.0.0 (GOV-651) directly from BZSt's live 'Erneute Mitteilung der IdNr' online form (driven end-to-end with a headless browser, not sourced from a PDF/prose description), the first National ID & Civic Documents candidate authored from a live-form walkthrough rather than a PDF or guidance page. Schema status 'verified' — see VERIFICATION.md.

candidates.title: United States Permanent Resident Card (Green Card) Renewal/Replacement (Form I-90)
candidates.agentTask: Renew or replace a lawful permanent resident's Green Card (expired, expiring, lost, stolen, damaged, or biographic-detail correction).
candidates.notes: Identified during the GOV-200 catalog gap review: high-frequency, single-form, well-bounded immigration-adjacent renewal with an online (myUSCIS account) and paper (mail to the Phoenix or Tempe, AZ lockbox) channel, comparable in shape to us/ssa/social-security-card-replacement-ss5 and us/uscis/change-of-address-ar11 (both already published). Distinct from us/uscis/naturalization-n400 (still candidate, tier 3): I-90 does not touch eligibility/adjudication, only card renewal/replacement for an existing permanent resident. Reason-for-application branches (expired/expiring, lost/stolen/destroyed, never received, biographic correction, commuter status, etc.) would need modelling as an enum plus conditional evidence, following the SS-5 pattern.

candidates.title: United States Application for Employment Authorization (Form I-765)
candidates.agentTask: Request an initial, renewal, or replacement/correction Employment Authorization Document (EAD) under one of USCIS's published eligibility categories.
candidates.notes: Assigned directly from GOV-664 plan §2 (F1.2), the federal passport/immigration authoring wave. Added directly at 'published' since it did not previously exist as a candidate. Very-high-volume, online-and-paper USCIS filing whose central complexity is the eligibility-category enumeration (Item Number 27): 53 literal codes (e.g. '(a)(3)', '(c)(3)(A)', '(c)(17)(iii)') were cross-derived from the companion Form I-765 Instructions' 'Who May File Form I-765' section, since the form itself only cross-references the Instructions rather than enumerating categories on its face. Authored in registry/us/uscis/employment-authorization-i765/1.0.0 from the live Form I-765 and Form I-765 Instructions PDFs at uscis.gov (both fetched directly, no access block; extracted via pdfjs-dist). Distinct from us/uscis/permanent-resident-card-replacement-i90 (checkbox-driven reason enum) and us/uscis/naturalization-n400 (still candidate): I-765's core branching is a large free-text-coded category selection, not a small checkbox set. Not time-versioned (GSP-0005 §2 coexistence test fails: filed per employment-authorization event, not a recurring period), so authored at the plain (non-edition) path. candidates.title: Germany National Identity Card Application/Renewal (Personalausweis)

Validity is 6 years under age 24, 10 years for 24+; the card also carries an optional eID/online-authentication function activated via a PIN letter. Authored GOV-730: sourced from Berlin's live service page plus BMI personalausweisportal.de Wayback captures and the Wikipedia field-layout cross-check; closes the last open tier-1 National ID & Civic Documents candidate.

candidates.title: Singapore Identity Card (NRIC) Loss/Damage Replacement

Replacement fee escalates for repeat losses (S$300 for a 2nd+ loss) — a fee fact excluded from fields, not modelled (see paymentMethod instead). The primary Singpass e-Service could not be entered, so fields were grounded in the official PDF form, cross-corroborated by a live public FormSG endpoint; scoped to self-application only (proxy/medical-incapacity reporting and age-30/55 re-registration are out of scope). candidates.title: India Fresh Passport Application (Ordinary, Adult, Citizen by Birth)

authority.name: Ministry of External Affairs, Government of India (Passport Seva / Regional Passport Office)

candidates.documentRef: Passport Application Form and Supplementary Form — Instructions Booklet V3.0 (Ministry of External Affairs)
candidates.agentTask: Prepare an adult Indian citizen-by-birth applicant's first (never previously issued) ordinary passport application ahead of submission through the Passport Seva Online Portal and the in-person verification appointment at a Passport Seva Kendra or other collection centre.
candidates.notes: Authored during the GOV-869 'GovSchema Standard Research' cycle 2026-07-03: the 10-jurisdiction x 6-vertical (DMV, Business Formation, Visa, Passport, Taxes, National ID & Civic Documents) matrix established across prior cycles was found saturated at 59/60 (only nl/Visa and eu/ETIAS gaps remain, both already investigated and deemed infeasible/not-yet-live in GOV-859 and GOV-756/GOV-822 respectively), so this cycle opened India as an 11th jurisdiction instead, following the same routine-expansion precedent as GOV-829 (NL as 10th). India was chosen for its scale and English-language official documentation (Ministry of External Affairs publishes its instruction booklet, annexures, and category/document tables directly in English). Scoped to Case I(A)(1) of the MEA's own applicant-category table — an adult, citizen-of-India-by-birth, first-time (never-previously-held-a-passport) applicant — the same 'first adult' scoping convention used by fr/ants, gb/hmpo, ca/ircc, and au/apo's passport-application-first-adult schemas. Out of scope, and left for future GOV-869-style cycles: minor applicants (Case I(B), guardian-consent branch), re-issue of an existing/lost/damaged passport, citizenship by descent/registration/naturalization (Case I(A)(2)/(3)), and diplomatic/official passport holders. The instruction-booklet PDF uses embedded CID-keyed fonts that defeat the usual zlib-stream/Tj-paren-regex extraction technique (no poppler/pdftoppm available in this environment to rasterize it either); a scratch npm install of pdfjs-dist's legacy Node build, driven directly rather than through a browser, decoded the real glyph-to-Unicode mapping and extracted all 21 pages of column-by-column field guidance, the applicant-category table, and the fee list. Three enum fields (gender, marital status, employment type) were corroborated against a raw curl fetch of a secondary source (bankbazaar.com) since the primary booklet describes them narratively ('put a cross in the appropriate box') without listing the exact box-label strings; this is flagged honestly in the schema's own verification.notes as a lower-confidence corroboration than the primary-source-only fields. Authored v1.0.0 same cycle: registry/in/mea/passport-application-first-adult/1.0.0.

candidates.notes: GOV-1181 'GovSchema Standard Research' research cycle (2026-07-05): per GOV-1174's closing note, South Africa opened as the 13th jurisdiction with only one schema published (za/dha/passport-application-first-adult); every other ZA vertical remained unopened. A repeat candidates[] audit again found only the same two dead ends open within the six focus verticals (ca/on/mto/vehicle-permit-renewal, blocked on a live plate/permit eligibility lookup; eu/etias, still pre-launch), so this cycle expanded the new ZA jurisdiction instead, opening Business Formation. Sourced from four genuine text-layer CIPC PDF forms (CoR14.1, CoR14.1A, CoR9.1, CoR15.1A) plus CIPC's own supporting-documents/fee-schedule reference table, all fetched directly from cipc.co.za. Authored in registry/za/cipc/private-company-incorporation/1.0.0, scoped to a single founder who is sole incorporator and sole initial director, filing electronically with the statutory standard-form MOI (CoR15.1A) rather than a customised MOI. A mock conformance/za/cipc/private-company-incorporation/1.0.0/application-packet.json test run, checked with a from-scratch GSP-0013 condition-grammar evaluator plus 3 mutation tests, passed with 0 violations across 26 fields (19 collected, 7 correctly not-applicable). Schema status 'draft' pending a live e-Services/BizPortal screen-walkthrough confirmation — see VERIFICATION.md.

candidates.title: South Africa Application for a Driving Licence (Form DL1)

authority.name: Road Traffic Management Corporation, Republic of South Africa

candidates.agentTask: Apply for a first (new) Code B driving licence at a Driving Licence Testing Centre in South Africa, using Form DL1.
candidates.notes: GOV-1194 'GovSchema Standard Research' cycle: a per-vertical audit of all 13 published jurisdictions found South Africa (added across GOV-1174/1181/1187/1190) still only 3/6 focus verticals (Passport, Business Formation, National ID) with zero DMV, Visa, or Taxes candidates in the catalog. Opens the DMV vertical for ZA. Authored in registry/za/rtmc/driving-licence-application/1.0.0 from Form DL1(18)(2005/11) — natis.gov.za's own forms page 403-blocks direct fetch (same WAF-block class as i-kfz.de/mca.gov.in), so the form was sourced from a municipal .gov.za mirror (nelsonmandelabay.gov.za) with a genuine text layer, cross-checked verbatim against a second independent mirror (weq4u.co.za) — plus gov.za's own 'Apply for a driving licence' page for the learner's-licence prerequisite, required documents, and fee guidance not on the form itself. Scoped to the new_driving_licence transaction, Code B (light motor vehicle) only; the form's other transaction types (government-department-licence conversion, foreign-licence replacement, replacement in ID document/paper card, temporary licence, new card/duplicate, and the two notice-of-change transactions) and other vehicle codes are out of scope. No fixed national application fee was found (fees are set per DLTC/province), so `applicationFee` is modeled without a fixed amount. A mock conformance/za/rtmc/driving-licence-application/1.0.0/application-packet.json test run, checked with a scripted requiredWhen/enum/pattern conformance evaluator, passed the schema's own constraints (0 violations across 31 fields); the foreign-ID and prior-refusal requiredWhen branches were separately exercised by mutating the packet and confirming the evaluator correctly flags the newly-required field. Schema status 'draft' pending a live DLTC intake walkthrough or an online.natis.gov.za (Gauteng) booking-flow confirmation; see VERIFICATION.md. Remaining ZA gaps for a future cycle: Visa (South Africa does operate an eVisa system at ehome.dha.gov.za, but it is fully login-gated with no downloadable guidance found this cycle) and Taxes (SARS eFiling ITR12 — the Comprehensive Guide PDF and a Treasury-hosted static ITR12 form both exist and looked sourceable, but were not pursued this cycle in favor of the DMV gap). Review gate GOV-1196 independently re-extracted Form DL1's text layer and found one field, `facsimileNumber` (Section A, 'Facsimile number'), present on the form but missing from the initially authored set; added pre-merge, field count is now 32 (see VERIFICATION.md).

candidates.title: South Africa Individual Income Tax Return (ITR12), 2026 year of assessment
candidates.agentTask: Prepare a South African resident individual's ITR12 income tax return for the 2026 year of assessment: the Form Wizard customisation questions, taxpayer/contact/address details, bank account details, and declaration.
candidates.notes: GOV-1201 'GovSchema Standard Research' cycle: per GOV-1194's closing note, South Africa had 4/6 focus verticals published (DMV, Passport, Business Formation, National ID) but no Taxes or Visa candidate, and that note specifically flagged SARS eFiling ITR12 as sourceable but not yet pursued. Opens the Taxes vertical for ZA (5/6). Authored in registry/za/sars/individual-income-tax-return-itr12/1.0.0 from SARS's own current Comprehensive Guide to the ITR12 (IT-AE-36-G05, Revision 40, effective 29 June 2026, written for the 2026 year of assessment) — a genuine text-layer PDF fetched directly from sars.gov.za and extracted with pdfjs-dist, no access block encountered. Scoped to the resident 'Standard Questions on Form Wizard' (Comprehensive Guide §4.1) plus the Taxpayer Information/Declaration/Bank Account Details sections (§5); out of scope, per the schema description: the 'Comprehensive Questions on Form Wizard' (§4.2 — non-residents, foreign income, capital gains, local farming) and every downstream, per-item repeating income/deduction detail grid a wizard answer dynamically adds (e.g. up to 20 rental activities or 20 trusts), since spec v0.3 has no repeating/nested field model (SPEC.md Section 12). Not authored against the v0.3 edition axis — `edition.scheme` is a closed enum (us-tax-year/gb-tax-year/award-year, GSP-0005) and extending it to a South African tax-year scheme is an open one-way-door proposal (GSP-0019) not made unilaterally here — following the same non-edition precedent as fr/dgfip/income-tax-return-2042 and de/finanzamt/income-tax-return-elster. A mock conformance/za/sars/individual-income-tax-return-itr12/1.0.0/application-packet.json test run (a married-in-community-of-property PAYE employee with medical/retirement-annuity contributions), independently checked with a from-scratch GSP-0013 condition evaluator plus a mutation test, passed with 0 violations across 78 fields (50 collected, 28 correctly not-applicable) and 10 documents (7 required, 3 correctly not-applicable). Schema status 'draft' pending a live sarsefiling.co.za wizard walkthrough (login-gated, not exercised this cycle); see VERIFICATION.md. Remaining ZA gap for a future cycle: Visa (South Africa's eVisa system at ehome.dha.gov.za remains fully login-gated with no downloadable guidance found across three cycles now).

candidates.title: South Africa Online Voter Registration (IEC VoterPortal)
candidates.documentRef: IEC Online Voter Registration Factsheet; IEC How to Register to Vote Factsheet
candidates.agentTask: Register to vote in-country on the IEC's online VoterPortal, or confirm/update registered address, ahead of South Africa's 2026 local government elections.
candidates.notes: GOV-1208 'GovSchema Standard Research' cycle: a fresh per-vertical audit found South Africa was the only one of this registry's 13 jurisdictions with no voter-registration schema at all (every other jurisdiction has one published, or, for Singapore, a documented non-applicability finding, GOV-1075). CA's known ca/on/mto/vehicle-permit-renewal candidate was retried first and re-confirmed still blocked — services.ontario.ca's live wizard again returned 'You are not eligible to renew your licence plate online at this time... up to 180 days before it expires' without a genuine, already-issued Ontario plate/permit, the same wall GOV-960 hit. Pivoted to this ZA gap instead, since unlike ZA's Visa system (login-gated across three prior cycles, GOV-1174/1187/1201) the IEC's registertovote.elections.org.za VoterPortal is a public, unauthenticated Blazor app and was directly reachable. Authored in registry/za/iec/voter-registration/1.0.0: the first screen ('Identity verification' — ID-number input, Section 97/88 declaration checkbox, ID-document file upload) was captured directly from the live DOM via a headless-browser walkthrough, no bot-block encountered. A syntactically-valid mock SA ID number (correct Luhn check digit, no real citizen behind it) was rejected with an on-page 'ID number is invalid' message, confirming a real-time Home Affairs population-register lookup gates every later screen (name/surname, contact details, OTP, address capture) — those fields were instead sourced from two official IEC PDF factsheets fetched directly (HTTP 200, pdfjs-dist text-layer extraction), consistent with how za/dha/smart-id-card-application and other identity-gated flows in this registry are handled. A mock conformance/za/iec/voter-registration/1.0.0/application-packet.json test run, independently checked with a from-scratch field-by-field validator plus two mutation tests (eligibility-declaration flip, ID-number pattern break — both correctly flagged), passed with 0 violations across 9/9 fields. Schema status 'draft' pending a live end-to-end walkthrough with a genuine registered SA ID number; see VERIFICATION.md. ca/on/mto/vehicle-permit-renewal remains open, now confirmed blocked across two cycles (GOV-960, GOV-1208) on the same real-plate/permit requirement — a future cycle should try a different sourcing angle (e.g. a ServiceOntario field-level user guide) rather than re-attempting the live wizard a third time.

candidates.title: India Re-Issue of Ordinary Passport (Adult)

authority.name: Ministry of External Affairs, Government of India (Passport Seva / Regional Passport Office)

candidates.documentRef: Passport Application Form and Supplementary Form — Instructions Booklet V3.0, Table 2(II) 'Re-issue of Passport'
candidates.agentTask: Re-issue an existing Indian ordinary passport — on expiry, exhaustion of visa pages, damage, loss/theft, or a change of personal particulars — for an adult applicant, including the common 'minor-to-major' transaction where a passport issued to a minor is reissued once its holder is an adult.
candidates.notes: GOV-1224/GOV-1227 'GovSchema Standard Research' follow-up: GOV-1224's catalog review found in/mea/passport-application-first-adult only covers fresh/first-time applications, leaving re-issue — the highest-volume real-world passport transaction, since every passport holder returns to it roughly every 10 years or sooner — unmodelled. Authored in registry/in/mea/passport-reissue/1.0.0 from the same MEA Instruction Booklet V3.0 PDF used by the first-adult schema (re-extracted with pdfjs-dist and searched specifically for its Table 2(II) 'Re-issue of Passport' content: Column 1.2's reissue-reason list, the 16-row case table spanning exhaustion/expiry/damage/loss-theft/15 change-of-particulars sub-cases, and the corresponding Table 3 document numbers), plus a live Playwright render of the Passport Seva Online Portal's public Document Advisor tool (services2.passportindia.gov.in/psp/docAdvisor/reissuePassport) — a genuine client-rendered React SPA invisible to a static fetch (confirmed via curl: raw HTML is an empty '#root' div). No MCP browser tool was available in this environment; a scratch script drove a local headless Chromium binary directly (LD_LIBRARY_PATH pointed at the sysroot's libglib to satisfy its shared-library dependency). The live Document Advisor confirmed the exact reissue question set and — materially — that proof of present address is conditional on an address change for reissue (unlike the fresh pathway, where it is unconditional); the live ApplyReissue informational page confirmed the Ordinary-vs-Diplomatic/Official routing boundary modelled as oldPassportType. A from-scratch condition-evaluator script exercised three positive profiles (plain expiry, lost/stolen government employee with changed address, marriage-name-change retired official) plus all three eligibility exits; every requiredWhen/transition branch resolved as intended. Schema status 'draft' pending a live authenticated-portal field-by-field pass (Login/Register wall not reached), consistent with the first-adult schema's own unresolved verification gap. Out of scope for this pass, flagged in the schema description: minor applicants still under 18 (Annexure D, a distinct schema), Diplomatic/Official passport reissue, Short-Validity-Passport renewal and the going-abroad-for-studies document variant (narrow edge cases), and document-level modelling of 11 of the 15 change-of-particulars sub-cases (only marriage/divorce name change, spouse-name addition, and appearance change are modelled at the document level) — see VERIFICATION.md.

candidates.title: India Driving Licence Application (New)

authority.name: Ministry of Road Transport and Highways, through state Regional Transport Offices (RTOs)

candidates.agentTask: Apply for the issue of a new (permanent) Driving Licence at an Indian RTO after already holding a valid Learner's Licence for the same class, via the state Sarathi portal or in person.
candidates.notes: GOV-1240 research: Phase 1 catalog re-audit found CATALOG.md stale (it still listed IN/NL/NZ as missing Passport, already closed by GOV-1227/prior NL and NZ passport schemas); corrected separately. With the catalog corrected, all 12 tracked jurisdictions cover all 6 focus verticals except two confirmed dead-end NL/ZA Visa gaps (GOV-777, GOV-1225), so this cycle targeted a sub-process gap within an already-covered vertical instead: in/morth/learners-licence-application (GOV-878) explicitly scoped out 'Issue of New Driving Licence' as one of Form 2's six other services; this candidate closes it. A prior blocked cycle (GOV-1215) had queued a US federal CDL schema on the mistaken premise that CDL coverage was 'completely new' in the registry, when us/ca/dmv/commercial-drivers-license-application already existed; that work was not used (see VERIFICATION.md). Sourced from the same canonical Form 2 PDF already used for the LL schema (re-fetched and re-extracted independently this cycle), plus parivahan.gov.in's own fee schedule (Rs. 200 issue-of-DL fee, distinct from the LL's Rs. 150). sarathi.parivahan.gov.in remains WAF-blocked. A mock conformance/in/morth/driving-licence-application/1.0.0/application-packet.json test run, checked with a scratch script re-implementing the schema's own required/requiredWhen/validation/eligibleValues logic across 4 scenarios (baseline, transport-vehicle happy path, transport-vehicle missing-document negative control, wrong-service negative control), passed with 0 violations on the two happy-path scenarios and correctly flagged both negative controls. Schema status 'draft' pending a live Sarathi state-portal field-by-field pass.
